page: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64 and includes 480 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core testing, as well as in software compilation, image processing, and text processing. Redis SET operations reached a top-tier throughput of 120.37 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput was measured at 105.57 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests showed strong read-only performance at 5123.16 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 192.43 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth was average at 76.62 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i.16xlarge offers high memory density and advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for cryptography and integer operations. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires reliance on external network storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. The instance is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, high-throughput database operations, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring a dedicated 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz and 512.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, which reach 120.37 million operations per second. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it provides a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-bound workloads, database operations, and text processing, though users must provision external storage for data persistence.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized Nitro instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier Redis and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized Nitro instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring sixty-four vCPUs and DDR5 RAM for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory.
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 480 MB L3 cache and AMX support.
Top-tier Redis performance reaching 120.37 million operations per second.
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage or bundled GPUs, requiring external storage solutions.
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and text processing.
